refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_overhead_budget in cycle time impact.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and travel speed within safety limits; allocate cycle time budgets to load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; implement closed-loop control with feedback.
DP-2.B: Minimize teleoperation network latency via low-latency communication protocol and edge processing; implement predictive operator input buffering.
DP-2.C: Design safety services to execute safety checks in parallel with primary control path; use safety-critical fast-path logic to minimize timing overhead.
DP-2.D: Implement real-time telemetry collection and p95 cycle time calculation on vehicle or edge gateway; expose cycle time KPI via Telemetry interface.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-2: Decompose cycle time performance into vehicle actuation/control optimization (DP-2.A), teleoperation latency minimization (DP-2.B), safety overhead reduction (DP-2.C), and telemetry-based KPI monitoring (DP-2.D). Allocate cycle time budgets to each sub-system and enforce via closed-loop feedback and real-time monitoring.

potential_sources: Vehicle actuation speed vs. safety constraint interaction: aggressive acceleration may trigger safety limits, creating coupling between DP-2.A and DP-2.C.
Teleoperation latency vs. vehicle control responsiveness: high latency may require operator to reduce speed, coupling DP-2.B and DP-2.A.
Telemetry overhead: real-time KPI calculation may consume compute resources needed for control, coupling DP-2.D with DP-2.A and DP-2.B.
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are manageable via explicit budget allocation and trade-off analysis. Recommend sensitivity analysis on latency_budget and safety_overhead_budget during detailed design.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/dynamic limits and safety constraints. Risk: If CT_target is unrealistic, no DP can satisfy FR-2.
Assumption: Teleoperation network can deliver latency ≤ latency_budget consistently. Risk: Network congestion or infrastructure limitations may violate latency budget.
Assumption: Safety services can be optimized to < safety_overhead_budget. Risk: Safety-critical logic may require timing overhead that exceeds budget, forcing cycle time trade-off.
Assumption: Telemetry collection does not significantly impact vehicle control performance. Risk: Real-time KPI calculation on resource-constrained vehicle may degrade control responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-2 to explicitly define cycle time budget allocation (e.g., load: 30s, travel: 45s, dump: 20s, safety overhead: 5s) and latency budget for teleoperation (e.g., ≤200ms round-trip). Specify telemetry sampling rate and KPI calculation method (e.g., rolling p95 over 100-cycle window) to avoid ambiguity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration boundary encompassing vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services. Responsible for cycle time KPI achievement, budget allocation across sub-systems, and end-to-end telemetry collection and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle time KPI data (p95, p50, min, max), sub-cycle durations (load, travel, dump), latency events, safety intervention events, vehicle state (speed, position, load mass). Published to Site Manager and System Engineer for monitoring and analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Operator or autonomous system commands to initiate load→travel→dump cycles. Includes cycle parameters (destination, load type, priority). Consumed by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety events and constraints that impact cycle time (e.g., speed limit reduction, emergency stop, geofence violation). Published by Safety Services; consumed by Vehicle control to enforce timing constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for teleoperation commands and telemetry data. Latency and bandwidth constraints directly impact cycle time performance. Managed by Teleoperation module.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. Cycle time KPI is a system-level outcome that requires orchestration across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Containment is high because the DP (budget allocation, closed-loop monitoring, telemetry collection) is naturally owned at the system level. Minimal diffusion: only telemetry collection logic and KPI calculation may be distributed to Vehicle or edge gateway, but orchestration remains at NGHE Solution level.

Vehicle owns actuation and control (DP-2.A) and contributes significantly to cycle time performance. However, Vehicle cannot independently achieve FR-2 because cycle time depends on teleoperation latency (DP-2.B) and safety constraints (DP-2.C), which are outside Vehicle scope. Diffusion risk: if Vehicle is assigned full responsibility for cycle time KPI, it will be forced to own telemetry collection and KPI calculation, creating tight coupling with Teleoperation and Safety Services.

Teleoperation owns latency minimization (DP-2.B) and is critical to cycle time performance. However, Teleoperation cannot independently achieve FR-2 because cycle time also depends on vehicle actuation (DP-2.A) and safety constraints (DP-2.C). Diffusion risk: if Teleoperation is assigned full responsibility, it will be forced to own vehicle control optimization and safety constraint management, violating separation of concerns.

Safety Services owns safety overhead reduction (DP-2.C) but is not responsible for overall cycle time KPI. Assigning FR-2 to Safety Services would create high diffusion: Safety Services would need to own vehicle actuation and teleoperation latency management, which are outside its domain. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution owns the cycle time KPI requirement and delegates sub-requirements (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and itself respectively. NGHE Solution is responsible for: (a) defining and allocating cycle time budgets to sub-systems, (b) collecting telemetry from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, (c) calculating p95 cycle time KPI, (d) monitoring compliance with CT_target, and (e) triggering corrective actions if KPI is violated.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may lack direct control over Vehicle actuation timing, making it difficult to enforce cycle time budgets. Mitigation: Define clear cycle time budget allocation and implement feedback control loop that adjusts vehicle speed/acceleration based on real-time cycle time tracking.

Risk: Teleoperation latency may be unpredictable due to network conditions, making cycle time KPI difficult to guarantee. Mitigation: Implement latency monitoring and operator feedback; design vehicle control to adapt to variable latency.

Risk: Safety constraints may impose timing overhead that exceeds safety_overhead_budget, forcing cycle time trade-off. Mitigation: Optimize safety services to minimize overhead; define acceptable trade-off between safety and cycle time performance.

Risk: Telemetry collection and KPI calculation may consume significant compute resources, impacting vehicle control responsiveness. Mitigation: Implement efficient telemetry collection (e.g., event-based sampling) and offload KPI calculation to edge gateway or cloud.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine IM assignment to explicitly define: (1) Cycle time budget allocation (load, travel, dump, safety overhead); (2) Telemetry data model and collection points (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (3) KPI calculation method and location (Vehicle, edge gateway, or cloud); (4) Feedback control loop for cycle time monitoring and corrective action; (5) Interface contracts between NGHE Solution and sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) for cycle time budget enforcement.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-2 (decomposition into vehicle actuation optimization, teleoperation latency minimization, safety overhead reduction, and telemetry-based KPI monitoring) directly addresses FR-2 (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). The DP is logically sound and covers all major performance drivers. Coupling between DP elements is manageable via budget allocation and trade-off analysis.

diffusion_risks: Risk: If Vehicle is assigned full responsibility for cycle time KPI, it will be forced to own teleoperation latency and safety constraint management, creating high diffusion and tight coupling.
Risk: If Teleoperation is assigned full responsibility, it will be forced to own vehicle actuation optimization and safety constraint management, violating separation of concerns.
Risk: If Safety Services is assigned full responsibility, it will be forced to own vehicle actuation and teleoperation latency management, which are outside its domain.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it is the system-level integration point where Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are orchestrated. Assigning FR-2 to NGHE Solution avoids diffusion and maintains clear separation of concerns. Sub-requirements (FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4) can be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively without creating coupling or diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-2 is verifiable via measurement of p95 cycle time across representative duty cycles. Telemetry interface provides necessary data (cycle timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) for root cause analysis. Verification requires controlled test environment with agreed duty cycle and site conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-2 is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution with sub-requirements allocated to Vehicle (FR-2.1), Teleoperation (FR-2.2), Safety Services (FR-2.3), and NGHE Solution (FR-2.4). Allocation is unambiguous and avoids overlap or gaps.

recommended_revisions: Refine FR-2 to explicitly define CT_target value, duty cycle parameters (load mass, travel distance, terrain type, operator skill level), and site conditions (ambient temperature, visibility, network coverage).
Refine DP-2 to specify cycle time budget allocation (e.g., load: 30s, travel: 45s, dump: 20s, safety overhead: 5s) and latency budget for teleoperation (e.g., ≤200ms round-trip).
Refine IM assignment to define telemetry data model, KPI calculation method, and feedback control loop for cycle time monitoring and corrective action.
Conduct sensitivity analysis on latency_budget and safety_overhead_budget to identify critical performance drivers and trade-offs.

decision.justification: FR-2 is a system-level KPI requirement that depends on multiple sub-systems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Decomposition into sub-requirements is necessary to allocate responsibility and enable detailed design. DP-2 provides a clear decomposition strategy. NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for orchestrating cycle time performance across sub-systems.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets (load: 30s, travel: 45s, dump: 20s).
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ 200ms (round-trip) to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding 5s safety overhead in cycle time impact.

FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events, safety interventions) and calculate p95 cycle time KPI for verification.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle kinematic/dynamic limits and safety constraints.
Teleoperation network can deliver latency ≤ 200ms consistently under agreed site conditions.
Safety services can be optimized to < 5s safety overhead without compromising safety.
Telemetry collection and KPI calculation do not significantly impact vehicle control performance.
Operator skill level and training are adequate to achieve cycle time targets.
Duty cycle parameters (load mass, travel distance, terrain type) are representative of customer use cases.
open_risks: Risk: CT_target may be unrealistic, requiring trade-off with safety or cost. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study with customer to validate CT_target against vehicle capabilities and site conditions.
Risk: Teleoperation latency may be unpredictable due to network congestion or infrastructure limitations. Mitigation: Implement latency monitoring and adaptive control to handle variable latency.
Risk: Safety constraints may impose timing overhead that exceeds 5s budget. Mitigation: Optimize safety services; define acceptable trade-off between safety and cycle time.
Risk: Telemetry collection may consume significant compute resources. Mitigation: Implement efficient telemetry collection and offload KPI calculation to edge gateway or cloud.
Risk: Operator fatigue or skill variation may impact cycle time performance. Mitigation: Implement operator training and fatigue monitoring; consider autonomous operation for repetitive cycles.
evidence_needed: Customer-defined CT_target value and duty cycle parameters.
Vehicle kinematic/dynamic model and actuation response time specifications.
Teleoperation network latency measurements under agreed site conditions.
Safety services timing overhead analysis and optimization results.
Telemetry data model and KPI calculation method specification.
Cycle time measurement data from prototype or pilot deployment (≥100 cycles).
Sensitivity analysis on latency_budget and safety_overhead_budget.
Trade-off analysis between cycle time, safety, and cost.
